Governance-grade inspection and control layer for enterprise AI: agent blueprints (what agents do, tools and services called, data touched, identities and permissions), agentic identity governance, MCP server and key sprawl controls, AI cost management. 2026-06: capability flags backfilled from public sources (SecurityWeek, Access Newswire); shadow_ai_discovery rests on runtime discovery of the agent/MCP estate.

name: Apex Security

notes: Acquired by Tenable; technology integrated to add AI controls, user behavior observability, and policy enforcement to Tenable's exposure management platform

name: Capsule Security
key_capability: Enterprise AI agent security and runtime protection
notes: Israeli startup focused on securing enterprise AI agents; raised $7M seed round led by Lama Partners and Forgepoint Capital. Runtime layer that monitors agent reasoning, interactions, and execution; blocks risky commands, tools, and exposures. Supports Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot Studio, ServiceNow, and Salesforce Agentforce; released open-source ClawGuard (pre-execution checkpoint for agent tool calls). 2026-06: capability flags backfilled from public sources (SecurityWeek, SiliconANGLE); pending human review.

name: Copperhelm
key_capability: Agentic cloud security with AI-driven workload analysis
notes: Agentic cloud security platform; emerged from stealth with $7M seed led by TLV Partners (toDay Ventures, ICON, SaaS Ventures Israel participating). Specialized AI agents for network analysis, system behavior, adversary simulation, and automated remediation across cloud workloads; connects to live workloads and deploys protections such as WAF rules. Founded by Shimon Tolts, Eyar Zilberman, Roman Labunsky. 2026-06: capability flags backfilled from public sources (PR Newswire, SecurityWeek). Review note: this is AI agents doing security rather than securing AI — soc_xdr is the core flag; ai_red_teaming rests on the adversary-simulation agents.

name: General Analysis
key_capability: Agentic AI deployment safety and governance platform
notes: Helps enterprises safely deploy agentic AI systems; raised $10M seed led by Altos Ventures

name: Koi
key_capability: Agentic AI security and autonomous agent risk management
notes: Agentic AI security startup acquired by Palo Alto Networks for ~$400M (completed 2026-04-14); integrated into Prisma AIRS and Cortex XDR. Agentic endpoint security: secures coding agents and autonomous endpoint tools that act with broad privileges across systems. Founded 2024 by 8200 alumni; raised $48M ($38M Series A) before acquisition. 2026-06: capability flags backfilled as of acquisition date (Apex Security precedent — acquired vendors retain flags; Aim Security by contrast was zeroed — policy decision pending).

name: Surf AI
key_capability: Agentic AI risk discovery and autonomous vulnerability remediation
notes: Agentic security operations platform; emerged from stealth 2026-03-17 with $57M led by Accel (Cyberstarts, Boldstart participating). Builds a context graph from identity, security, cloud, data, HR, and IT signals; AI agents prioritize risk by business impact and coordinate remediation under human oversight. Founded 2024 by Yair Grindlinger, Elad Horn, et al. 2026-06: capability flags backfilled from public sources (SecurityWeek, SiliconANGLE); HQ corrected Israel -> New York per same sources. Flags follow the Zafran precedent (risk-based remediation -> ai_spm + soc_xdr); this is AI-for-security rather than securing-AI — categorization worth review.

Founded by Eno Thereska (ex-AWS/Confluent), Neil Lawrence (Cambridge), Zhenwen Dai. 2026-06: capability flags backfilled from public sources (SecurityWeek, BusinessWire); ai_governance rests on explicit agent-governance positioning, not a shipped compliance module.

Pre-seed co-led by Sovereign's Capital and Chingona Ventures, December 2025. Positions as "AI judging AI" for shadow AI detection, policy enforcement, and SOC 2 compliance support.

name: Confident Security
key_capability: Privacy and data-loss layer for AI interactions — wraps AI models to guarantee data privacy
notes: Came out of stealth with $4.2M seed funding

capability_assessment.rationale: CONFSEC is a confidential / provably-private AI inference layer (an enterprise build of Apple's Private Cloud Compute) — prompts and metadata stay unseen and unstored at inference via OHTTP, blind signatures, remote attestation, TEEs/TPMs, and transparency logs. This is data-in-use privacy at inference time and maps to none of the 10 current capability dimensions. Closest adjacency is dspm_ai_data_sec, but DSPM covers data discovery and classification at rest — a different problem — so it stays false. Candidate trigger for an 11th dimension (confidential_inference) if more TEE-attestation inference vendors emerge.

name: Harmonic Security
key_capability: DLP for AI tools; shadow AI discovery
notes: Sequoia-backed

name: LayerX
funding_stage: Acquisition pending (Akamai, ~$205–250M reported, not closed; expected close Q3 2026)
key_capability: AI-powered enterprise browser security with shadow AI discovery and data-loss prevention for GenAI usage
notes: Akamai/LayerX acquisition reported but NOT closed. SecurityWeek (June 2026) reported an agreement at ~$205M all-cash; Calcalist (Ctech, June 2026) reports advanced negotiations at ~$250M — sources disagree on price. Status stays NEW (deal pending) until close is confirmed; do not mark ACQUIRED/EXITED for an unclosed deal. LayerX specializes in enterprise browser security with AI controls (shadow AI discovery, GenAI DLP). Was on the adjacent-incumbent watchlist. Acquirer (Akamai) is not a tracked vendor.

name: Menlo Security
key_capability: Browser-delivered GenAI and AI-agent governance — shadow AI discovery, browser DLP, and policy enforcement for human and agent AI use
notes: Browser-security incumbent (secure enterprise browser / remote browser isolation heritage) that pivoted into AI security, delivering GenAI controls at the browser layer: shadow AI discovery and real-time visibility, browser DLP that blocks risky uploads and prompt entries, and guidance toward sanctioned tools. Launched a Browser Security Platform to govern AI agents — human and non-human — in March 2026, adding agent-level prompt-injection prevention and forensic visibility. Acquired Votiro for content disarm and reconstruction and AI-driven data security.
